VACAVILLE CONVALESCENT & REHAB

CCN: 555349 · VACAVILLE, CA 95687 · Solano County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesPenaltiesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
585 NUT TREE COURT, VACAVILLE, CA 95687
Phone
7074498000
Certified beds
120
Avg daily residents
92 (76% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1989-04-20
Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 5.59 3.70 5.84 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.98 0.65 1.02 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.07 0.82 1.11 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.54 2.23 3.70 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 2.04 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.19 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.94
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.79
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
5.16
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.31
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-12-06 8 8 0 40 1 40
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-12-16 8 4 4 56 1 56
Total weighted health score
44.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (29)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
28 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-12-06 2025-01-03
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-12-06 2025-01-03
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-12-06 2025-01-03
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2024-12-06 2025-01-03
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2024-12-06 2025-01-03
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-12-06 2025-01-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-12-06 2025-01-03
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. E 2024-12-06 2025-01-03
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-09-30 2024-10-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-04-10 2024-05-31
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2024-02-28 2024-03-25
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-02-28 2024-03-25
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. E 2022-12-16 2023-01-19
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2022-12-16 2023-01-19
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2022-12-16 2023-01-19
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2022-12-16 2023-01-19
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. E 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2019-05-17 2019-06-17
